mainEntity.name: When should I replace my AC unit in Florida?

acceptedAnswer.text: Replace it when the unit is 10–15 years old, uses R-22 refrigerant, or needs a repair costing more than half the price of a new system. Frequent breakdowns, rising bills, and weak cooling are clear signs to replace before summer.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to replace an AC unit in Fort Lauderdale?

acceptedAnswer.text: AC unit replacement in Fort Lauderdale typically costs $5,500–$12,000 installed, depending on size, SEER2 efficiency, and ductwork. A standard 3-ton home system usually runs about $7,000–$9,500 including permit and old-unit removal.

mainEntity.name: How much does a new AC system cost in Broward County?

acceptedAnswer.text: A complete new AC system in Broward County generally costs $6,000–$12,000+ installed. High-efficiency variable-speed systems cost more up front but lower summer electric bills. Financing spreads the cost into low monthly payments.

mainEntity.name: How long does an AC installation take in Fort Lauderdale?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most residential AC installations take 4–8 hours for a standard changeout. Jobs adding ductwork, an air handler, or a high-efficiency system can take a full day. We often schedule same-week installs across Broward County.

mainEntity.name: Is it better to repair or replace my AC unit?

acceptedAnswer.text: If your unit is under 10 years old and the repair is minor, repair it. If it is past 12 years, on R-22, or facing a repair over half a new system's cost, replacement saves more long term. We provide both numbers in writing.
